observations.recommendation:
1.7 We recommended and Management agreed to require the following:
a. The City General Services Office (CGSO) to submit the SSMIs for the
previously issued inventories and, henceforth, prepare weekly the SSMI
based on the RIS for submission to the City Accounting Office as its
basis in preparing the JEV to record the supplies and materials issued;
and
b. The City Accountant to adjust the Other Supplies and Materials Inventory
account for the unrecorded issuances of supplies and materials based on
the SSMIs submitted by the GSO.

2.7 We recommended that the City Accountant apply the capitalization threshold
in recognizing PPE accounts and reclassify to semi-expendable properties/
expensed/charge to Prior Period Adjustments the PPE that are below the
capitalization threshold of ₱50,000.00 per item/unit in conformity with Item
3.7 of COA Circular No. 2024-006 dated March 14, 2024.

2.8 The City Accountant had already reclassified the semi-expendable properties
under Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-25-04-18 dated April 30, 2025 as
recommended.

3.6 We recommended that the CMEPO furnish the Accounting Office, together
with the documents relating to the Deed of Donation for them to recognize
the fair value of the vehicle at the date the value is determined.

3.7 During the exit conference, the CMEPO informed that an Acknowledgement
Receipt for Equipment (ARE) was prepared and the donated vehicle was already
insured with the GSIS, having a market value of ₱1,700,000.00. The documents of
ownership were also furnished to the Accounting Office for recording.

3.8 Journal Entry Voucher (JEV) No. 101-25-04-19 dated April 30, 2025 was prepared
by the City Accountant to recognize the fair value of the donated vehicle as
recommended.

4.4 We reiterated our recommendation and the CAO agreed with the OCAS/CVO
to reconcile their records at least quarterly and make the necessary
adjustments in the books to fairly present the affected accounts in the City’s
financial report.

7.13 We recommended that management strictly comply with the prescribed
requirements and supporting documents for request for relief from
accountability as provided under COA Memorandum No. 92-751 dated
February 24, 1992.

7.14 We recommended that the Legal Officer require the persons responsible to
submit documents/ return slips and written explanations to prove why they
should not be held personally liable for the missing properties issued to
them pursuant to Section 491 of GAAM, Volume 1.

7.15 We further recommended that Management issue PPEs or semi-expendable
properties only to permanent employees and require the JOs or COS
personnel to immediately surrender all issued PPEs and semi-expendable
properties to the CGSO and facilitate the cancellation of the Acknowledgement
Receipt for Equipment/Property Acknowledgement Receipt and the Inventory
Custodian Slip.

7.16 We further recommended that Management require the City GSO to account
properly the missing PPEs and strictly implement proper verification of
property accountabilities before clearance are issued to retiring/resigning
personnel.

7.17 Management committed to comply with the recommendations.

8.5. We recommended and the City Accountant agreed with the CGSO to maintain
SLCs and SCs, respectively, following Appendices 7 and 36 of the MNGAS
for LGUs, Volume II.

8.6. We also recommended and Management agreed to require that physical
count of inventory items be conducted by type semestrally and reported in
the RPCI to be reconciled quarterly with the SLC and/or SC and control
accounts and any discrepancy/ies be immediately verified and adjusted.

10.5. We recommended and Management agreed to only grant cash advance in
cases when it is impractical or impossible to make payments by check
and refrain from granting the same for activities already commenced or
occurred.

26.5 We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to prepare the annual
report on SEF utilization to enable the City Local School Board to submit
such report, quarterly and annual, to the concerned offices within the
prescribed period for timely evaluation and monitoring of its utilization in
compliance with the pertinent provisions in Item 6.1 of the DepEd, DBM,
and the DILG Joint Circular No. 1, series of 2017.

We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to adjust the affected accounts in order to have faithful presentation of the Financial Statements of the City in compliance to the necessary provisions of COA Circular 2015-09 and the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) I.
observations.managementAction: We were informed that the Audit Team will continuously monitor Reiterated in Part II, report assured that the journal entries to adjust the affected accounts will be done before end of CY 2024.
observations.reason: City Accountant is still on the process of reconciling the amounts and awaiting the withdrawal in full of the loan granted to the City Government by the DBP. Nevertheless, the amount should have already been adjusted since it is already identified.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the CAO and the OCAS/CVO to reconcile their records at least quarterly and submit to the accounting office make the necessary adjustments in the books in order to fairly present the affected accounts in the City’s financial report.
observations.managementAction: Veterinary committed to the CVO for the reconciliation of accounts, hence, the validation report on the death of biological assets being used by the city government in the production of livestock inventory and the report affected by the African Swine Flu to be derecognized in the account.
observations.reason: The City is still in process; no report was submitted by the Veterinary / CVO; being affected by African Swine Flu.

observations.recommendation:
a. concerned departments/offices responsible for the distribution of supplies and materials submit SSMI attaching the RIS and/or ICS to the accounting office.
b. Accounting department to ensure that asset account should be established and recognized expense only upon submission of report/proof of distribution by the concerned departments in order to fairly present the balances of accounts in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Implementated; No SSMI was submitted by the concerned department to the accounting department. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II of this report.
observations.reason: The City was unable to record the supplies due to lack of records provided by the concerned department, hence, no adjustment or action was taken by the accounting department.

observations.managementAction: Implementing departments assisted NGOs. Reiterated in Observation No. 1, Part II of this report. Legal action: Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue.
observations.reason: Some NGOs/POs have liquidated their accounts. However, extent of monitoring done and reasons for non-liquidated were not quantified through report by management. No action yet was taken by the legal officer on the matter.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management implement LGUs shares in the collection of the excise tax to any programs enumerated in Section 14 of RA No. 11346 and the comply with the reportorial requirements thereof in accordance to the applicable Local Budget Memorandum.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; The Office of the City Agriculturist mentioned that the Administrator has already assigned them the task of developing a proposal for the utilization of the excise tax. They clarified that the proposal’s objective is to promote cultivating high-value crops.
observations.reason: City Agriculturist is currently exploring viable projects to be funded by the Tobacco Excise Tax; no final implementation yet.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management adopts electronic payroll system through the Automated Teller Machine (ATM) of Government Banks for the payment of wages of JO workers and casual employees.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Only payment of salaries of casual personnel was paid through ATM. For implementation this year for the payment of wages of JOs.
observations.reason: Only salaries of casual personnel were paid through ATM; further implementation for JOs ongoing.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and management agreed to include all the unexpended prior year balances in its LDRRMFIP in accordance to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2012-02 dated September 12, 2012.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Audit Team will continuously monitor the actions undertaken by Management to resolve the issue. Reiterated in No. 10, Part II of this report.
observations.reason: Management to include prior year balances; action being monitored.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the City Mayor require all individuals who are in custody of the vehicles, that have expired LTO registration and bearing private plate number to surrender/temporarily surrender them to the CMEPO to expedite the processing of their insurance, LTO registration and the change of LTO registered plate number from private to government plate, as the case maybe, in compliance with the provisions of the above regulations.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; A memorandum was issued to individuals who are assigned vehicles requesting their cooperation to surrender/submit documents required to the CMEPO in order to fast-track the registration of the motor vehicles assigned to them.
observations.reason: There are still plate numbers that are not converted to government plates; some vehicles of the city are yet to be converted.

observations.recommendation: Require surrender of extra vehicles and craft policy/guidelines on common use of motor vehicles.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; A meeting was conducted with the City General Services Office and City Motor and Equipment Pool Office to discuss policies regarding the rules and matter.
observations.reason: No formal policy yet crafted by the City government on the common use of motor vehicles that are aligned with DBM circulars.

observations.recommendation: Require the HoPE to act on the BAC resolutions recommending award in compliance to RIRR of RA No. 9184.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; The City Administrator contends that signing the notice of award signifies conformance to Section 37.1.2. Management acknowledges that proper procedure entails the mayor signing the BAC Resolution and issuing the notice of award. To facilitate easier reference, both the notice of award and BAC Resolution have been assigned numbers.

observations.recommendation: Observe provisions of Item 9, Annex H, 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184 for procurement of lease venue with meals and accommodation.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; LGU floated RFQs to at least 3 suppliers, however, those rated with lowest calculated quotation was not being rated in accordance with the technical specification for the lease of venue.
observations.reason: The Lessor with the Lowest Calculated Quotation was not rated in accordance with Appendix B of the guidelines for the procurement for the lease of venue.

observations.recommendation: a. include comparative evaluation in post-qualification; b. formulate policies on sufficiency and availability of equipment pledged by contractors.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; A meeting was conducted with the Technical Working Group. The issue on the number of projects the equipment pledged by the contractor was not given attention in the post-qualification evaluation by the TWG.
observations.reason: No specific policy crafted yet; issue on equipment sufficiency not fully addressed.

observations.recommendation: Comply with Annex A of the RIRR re engineering surveys; establish policy for actions during suspensions to minimize delays.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Management is now requiring landowner waivers before project implementation. The City will not implement the project if ROW problem not resolved.
observations.reason: No formal policy yet for suspension handling; actions underway to require waivers.

observations.recommendation: Ensure BAC secretariat submits contracts and supporting documents to Auditor within five days after perfection for timely review and rectification.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented; For CY 2023, submission of contracts and supporting documents were not within the 5-days after perfection of the contract. Management assured the audit team that said recommendation be fully implemented in CY 2024.
observations.reason: Submission not made within required timeframe during CY 2023; Management plans to implement in CY 2024.

observations.recommendation: a. Evaluate financial position before obtaining loans; b. Study DBP Omnibus Term Loan Agreement for prepayment and consider pre-termination using time deposits if warranted.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; In the exit conference, Management disclosed that the idle funds cannot be readily utilized as they were tied to the beginning balance of the supplemental budget and need to undergo appropriation process. The Finance Committee will discuss further with the Mayor.
observations.reason: Idle funds are not readily available due to appropriation process; further discussion planned.

observations.recommendation: Undertake one-time cleansing of PPE accounts per COA Circular 2020-006 and effect necessary accounting adjustments.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Management to complete physical inventory; physical counts to be completed before end of third quarter of CY 2024.
observations.reason: Physical inventory not yet completed; accounting adjustments pending until inventory concluded.

observations.recommendation: Undertake required procedures and compile documentation to support COA write-off request for dormant receivables totaling ₱1,384,261.52.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented; There are no requests submitted to the audit team for write off of dormant receivables as of December 31, 2023.

observations.recommendation: Submit Property Insurance Form to GSIS General Insurance Fund and allocate budget for premiums.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented; For buildings, all were already insured to the GSIS and most of the service vehicles insured. As of December 31, 2023, there are still properties of the City which are not yet insured with GSIS.
observations.reason: Some properties not yet insured; follow-up required.

observations.recommendation: Exhaust all means to process and facilitate registration and transfer of title of parcels of land.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Management is still in the process of gathering data for the transfer of 2 lots due to some adverse claim.
observations.reason: Two lots still not transferred due to adverse claim.

observations.recommendation: Demand collection of overdue cash advances and request write-off if uncollectible.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Collections were made through deduction from LGU and terminal leave; Management is in the process of gathering data for possible request for write-off.
observations.reason: Some overdue cash advances remain; process for possible write-off ongoing.

observations.recommendation: Accounting Office to derecognize unserviceable motor vehicles after disposal.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Some unserviceable vehicles were disposed in CY 2022, but not all.
observations.reason: Not all unserviceable vehicles disposed as of CY 2023.

observations.recommendation: Update ARE of all properties for easy monitoring and reconciliation.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; The City Government noted that not all AREs/PARs of PPEs were renewed. AREs transitioned to new form as provided in the New Government Accounting Manual for LGUs.
observations.reason: Not all AREs/PARs were renewed; transition to new forms impacted updates.

observations.recommendation: Require completion of description and cost segregation of road projects for recording and valuation.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; The City Engineering Office is formulating the Roads and Bridges Inventory System (RBIS); Inventory and costing of local road networks ongoing; Accounting Office awaiting inventory report.
observations.reason: Inventory and costing not yet completed due to insufficient survey personnel; accounting recognition pending.

observations.recommendation: Require audited financial statements/Tax Returns for business tax assessments and strengthen review/examination of declared gross receipts.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Validation on the correctness of the re-assessment cannot be done since no reassessment was made or random examination of books of accounts was conducted. Management referred to AAPSI basis and indicated re-assessment collected.
observations.reason: No reassessment or random examinations were conducted; accuracy of business tax collected could not be fully validated.

observations.recommendation: Allocate funds to complete unfinished infrastructure projects to avoid waste and restore usability.
observations.managementAction: Not Implemented; Only some of the projects were allocated funding during the year.
observations.reason: Not all projects were funded for completion in the year under review.

observations.recommendation:
a. For the City Accountant to:
i. effect the proposed adjusting entries in Annex H: Summary of
Proposed Adjusting Entries;
ii. record rechanneling projects as expense since they are not
permanent structure in the output works;
iii. record liquidated damages as income, if funded from general
fund or Due to NGAs/LGUs/GOCCs, as case maybe, if funded
from trust fund instead of recognizing as Other payable for the
payment of BAC Honoraria and overtime pay.
b. The City General Services Office and City Health Services Office to
provide their respective Summary of Supplies and Materials Issued
and Requisition Slip to the Accounting Office to be used as basis in
recording issuances of supplies and materials;
c. The City Accountant and the Designated Supply Officers (CGSO and
CHO) to reconcile their respective records on inventory;
d. The Inventory Committee to conduct physical count on all inventory
items and submit the resulting Report of Physical Count of
Inventories;
e. The City Motor and Equipment Pool Office to submit copy of the
Deed of Donation (DOD) to recipient barangays together with the
authority of the LCE from SP to enter into and sign the DOD and
Property Transfer Receipt.

We also recommended that management facilitate the transfer or
donation of the stated projects Annex G either to the schools where
they are constructed (in case the school maintains its own books) or to
the Department of Education – Tabuk City Division.

We recommended and management agreed to require the Accounting
Office to devise their accounting system and reclassify the non-current
other payables amounting to ₱6,141,564.33 to current other payables.

We recommended and management agreed to:
a. to open a new account for the 20% Development Fund;
b. require the accounting office to submit details or schedule of the
20% development fund amounting to ₱117,216,382.02; and
c. require the budget office to submit the details of the current and
prior years unimplemented 20% development fund as of December
31, 2023.

We recommended that the accountant avoid granting cash advances
near the end of the quarter or on the last working day of the quarter for
confidential funds intended within the quarter.

We further recommended and management agreed to ensure that cash
advances are used during the targeted period of program/ project
implementation.

We recommended and Management agreed to:
a. demand the AOs to liquidate all their cash advances with complete
supporting documents, except petty cash fund, at the end of the
year;
b. withhold the salaries of accountable officers who failed to liquidate
their cash advances before January 20 of the ensuing year; and
c. impose the penalty as provided in CSC MC No. 23, s. 2019 for those
accountable officers who fail to make any liquidation, whether partial
or full, of the cash advances within the prescribed period stated in
the formal demand letter.

We recommended and Management agreed to strictly enforce the period
of liquidation as prescribed above and require the immediate
refund/return of any excess amount thereof.

We recommended and Management agreed to require the Accounting
Office to:
a. return to the source agencies all remaining unexpended/unutilized
balances of fund transfers or make a request from the source
agencies for the transfer of the unexpended balance to the General
Fund if the disposition thereof has not been provided in the MOA or
agreement; and
b. exhaust all efforts to identify the source agency where the balance
of ₱18,007.54 emanated and return the unutilized fund. Else, direct
the City Accountant to review the status of the accounts and
observe the guidelines and procedures on the write off of Dormant
Payable accounts.

We recommended and management agreed to stop allocating items
used for administrative purposes in the program of works for projects
funded under the 20% development fund.

We recommended and Management agreed to utilize only the 70% for
calamity fund for activities related thereto. Henceforth, ensure to abide
with the provisions of NDRRMC, DBM and DILG Joint Memorandum
Circular 2013-1 and PD No. 1445.

In lieu of the foregoing, we recommended and management agreed to:
a. include in the annual LDRRMFIP all activities and projects that are
to be funded under the Special Trust Fund; and
b. direct the LDRRMC to identify DRRM activities that may be funded
from the unexpended amount of ₱14,158,616.48 and include the
same in the CY 2024 LDRRMFIP.

We recommended and management agreed to require:
a. the BAC Secretariat to include the Schedule of Procurement
Activities in the Invitation to Bid as guide in the procurement
conducted through public bidding and other modes of procurement;
b. the BAC members to follow within the allowable calendar days on
the procurement activities especially in the contract preparation
and signing; and
c. the BAC to strictly adhere to Annex C of the Revised IRR to avoid
delay in the implementation of the programs and activities of the
City LGU.

In lieu of the foregoing, we recommended that Management provide the
specific locations of projects in the appropriation ordinance enacting
the annual budget of the city government or when not possible, secure
prior authorization from the sanggunian before projects without specific
locations are implemented.

We recommended that Management:
a. revisit their programs on values formation tasked by the Offices
concern such as CBACRO, CDSWDO and POP COM, of the City
government to handle well the activities stated in Item 1.3 and 1.5
above; and
b. the City government to refrain from appropriating expenditures for
religious and private purpose and ensure compliance with Section
29(2), Article VI of the Philippine Constitution and Section 335,
Chapter IV of the Local Government Code.

We recommended that management continuously implement GAD PPAs
as shown in the GPB.

We recommended and management agreed to require the persons liable
to settle the audit suspensions, disallowances and charges in
accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 and the 2009 Revised Rules
of Procedure of the Commission on Audit.

We recommended that Management adjust the affected accounts in order to fairly present the Financial Statements in compliance to the provisions of COA Circular 2015-09 and the International Public Sector Accounting Standards (IPSAS) I.
observations.managementAction: Implemented JEV No. 101-23-01-08 and 401-23-04-18

observations.recommendation: We recommended and the City Accountant agreed to properly classify and record collections from the sale of bid documents to their appropriate account and likewise recognize as income any excess from the proceeds from sale of bid documents after payment of honoraria, overtime pay and other duly authorized expenses at the end of the year for fair presentation of accounts in the financial position of the LGU.
observations.managementAction: Implemented JEV No. 401-23-04-01

observations.recommendation: a. Deduct warranty security 1%–5% or obtain special bank guarantee; b. Ensure validity of performance security and adherence to warranty security provisions of 2016 Revised IRR.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Required warranty security was deducted from disbursement vouchers. Corresponding warranty security was submitted as proof that the project or goods procured are secured in case of default by the contractor.

observations.recommendation: Do not include rental for service vehicle as pay item in POW; charge to MOOE of City Engineering Office.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; The office will no longer include service vehicles as pay items in the Program of Work, instead, they charged them to the MOOE of the Engineering Department.

observations.recommendation: Inform COA Team within 24 hours of deliveries of procured items for inspection as per COA Circular 96-010.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Management provided invitation requesting the presence of COA to join in the inspection of the delivery of goods.
observations.reason: Management indicated COA inspection requests will be made within 24 hours of delivery; COA involvement was facilitated.

observations.recommendation: Institute appropriate controls for donations in kind to ensure proper receipt and enjoyment by beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; The City General Services Office assured the audit team that for CY 2023 and thereafter, recipients must present proof of identification and an authorization from the Punong Barangay prior to release of items at the GSO office.

observations.recommendation: Ensure procured meals and snacks conform to PO specifications; otherwise deem the transaction irregular.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; For CY 2023, meals and snacks served/delivered in particular activities showed evidences that the specification provided in the contracts were fully delivered.

observations.recommendation: City Engineering Office and CPDC to improve procurement planning and adopt strategies to fast track prior years' projects.
observations.managementAction: Implemented; Management directed the City Engineering Office and City Planning to prepare a catch-up plan on the implementation of the projects.

observations.recommendation: We recommended and Management agreed that the Accounting Office effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the balances of accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the one-time cleansing of PPE account balances be undertaken pursuant to COA Circular No. 2020-006 to establish actual existence of and accountability for PPE units and present accurate balances of PPE accounts in the financial statements.

We further recommended that the Accountant effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the balances of accounts in the financial statements.
We recommended and Management agreed that the Accountant effect the necessary adjustments to fairly present the balances of affected accounts in the financial statements.

We recommended and Management agreed that the required procedures be undertaken and that the appropriate documentation be accomplished to support a request from COA for authority to write-off the dormant accounts receivable amounting to ₱1,384,261.52, pursuant to COA Circular No. 2016-005.

We recommended and Management agreed that the PDAF balance of ₱1,317,457.02 be remitted to the Bureau of Treasury and the total unexpended balances of ₱364,455.19 from completed projects be returned to the respective fund source agencies.

We recommended and Management agreed to adopt policies and internal controls to regulate loan accommodations and other payroll deductions to ensure compliance with the minimum monthly net take home pay of ₱5,000.00 pursuant to Section 49 of the General Provisions of RA No. 11518.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Property Insurance Form be accomplished and submitted to the General Insurance Fund of the GSIS, for appropriate action, and a budget be allocated for the insurance premiums.

We recommended and Management agreed that the Local Finance Committee prepare and post the Report on Fund Utilization and Status of Implementation of PPAs under the BGCM in the LGU’s website and in at least three (3) conspicuous public places in the locality at the end of every month, in compliance with the reportorial requirements under items 4.2 and 4.3 of DBM LBC No. 125.
We recommended that management enforce prompt settlement of suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Sections 7.1.1 and 7.1.2 of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts.

We recommended that the City
Accountant:
a. Prepare and maintain Property,
Plant and Equipment (PPE)-Ledger
Cards for all public infrastructures
recognized in the books of
accounts indicating cost of each
component pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 2015-008, and 2016-004
which shall be the basis in computing
the related depreciation expenses; and
b. Ensure that derecognition of public
infrastructure accounts are properly
taken-up in the books of accounts prior
to the recording of their replacement
costs due to betterments or
improvements, in accordance with
PPSAS No. 17.
observations.managementAction:
The City Mayor directed the City
Accountant to assign one staff whose work is to
prepare and maintain PPE Ledger Cards and
ensure that depreciation and and/or
derecognition of public infrastructure
accounts are properly taken-up in the books of
accounts.
Reiterated in Part II, Observation No. 2 of
this report

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management implement all programs, activities,
and projects in accordance with its Approved Local Disaster Risk Reduction
and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to enhance the city’s
disaster preparedness and response capabilities in times of calamity.

The City directed the CDRRMO to prepare a catch-up plan for the
implementation of delayed CDRRM Projects.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Sangguniang Panglungsod and the City Mayor
re-assess or evaluate their plans and decisions on the operations of the city
government to ensure that government funds are utilized for programs,
projects, and activities that are most beneficial to the constituents.
observations.managementAction:
The City has prioritized to construct a hospital in light of the COVID 19
pandemic and the need for an evacuation center or sports complex.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. formulate clear, specific standard guidelines for hiring/rehiring personnel
on job order/ casual/ contract of service to include, among others, the
following: • Conditions that would warrant the need for hiring/ rehiring of job
order personnel; • procedures in hiring; • qualifications and other
requirements; • funding/applicable rates; • incorporate in the contract the
works to be performed, quantity and duration and submit quantified
accomplishment reports; and • requesting and approving authorities.
b. coordinate with the Department Heads, particularly the HRMO, to assess
and review the necessity of hiring job orders in order to maximize the
services of permanent employees and at the same time minimize expenses
on job order personnel.
observations.managementAction:
The City will comply with the recommendation and directed the Monitoring
and Evaluation Team to conduct trainings related to the same rules and
regulations.

observations.recommendation:
1.12 We recommended and the City Accounting Office and the City
General Service Office agreed:
a. to coordinate and reconcile their records stating therein the
complete description of the property; and
b. to record the current market values of the seventeen (17) lots to
reflect the true and correct balance of the Land account.

1.13 We further recommended that management:
a. exhaust all possible means to facilitate the registration and
transfer of title of the parcels of land; and
b. for succeeding procurement of lot, comply with the documentary
requirements before full payment to avoid purchases of doubtful
origin and ownership or problems in the transfer of title.

2.11 We recommended that the City Accountant:
a. Prepare and maintain Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE)-Ledger
Cards for all public infrastructures recognized in the books of
accounts indicating cost of each component pursuant to COA
Circular Nos. 2015-008, and 2016-004 which shall be the basis in
computing the related depreciation expenses; and
b. Ensure that derecognition of public infrastructure accounts are
properly taken-up in the books of accounts prior to the recording of
their replacement costs due to betterments or improvements, in
accordance with PPSAS No. 17.

3.8 We recommended that management comply with the Price Act Law
and DOH/DTI Memorandum Circulars in the procurement of goods,
medical supplies and equipment for the prevention of COVID19 to
avoid excessive and/or unnecessary expenses and giving unwarranted
benefits to suppliers.

4.5 We recommended that Management carefully check and review
documents supporting every transaction to ensure the propriety,
legality and validity of disbursements/expenditures.

5.7 We recommended that management always prepare the
Summary/List of Donations Received, Distributed and Balances for
transparency that the goods received were properly accounted by the
City. Likewise, submit the same within ten (10) working days after the
end of each quarter to the NDRRMC, though the OCD and copy
furnished the COA Auditors in accordance with COA Circular No.
2020-009 dated April 21, 2020 for monitoring and evaluation.

6.4 We recommended that administrative expenses be charged to the
appropriation of the General Fund and return back to the LDRRM
fund the said amount with corresponding adjusting entries.

7.9 We recommended that the BAC and/or TWG carefully examine and
verify all the documents and re-compute the Bid Prices and Bill of
Quantities provided by the bidder to determine whether they comply
and are responsive as to amounts and automatically disqualify the
same if the bid is higher than the ABC.

8.5 We recommended that the City Engineer ensure that cost estimates
used in the preparation of the Program of Works (POW) of all
infrastructure projects are in accordance with the existing applicable
Department of Public Works and Highways (DPWH) standards and
guidelines in the preparation of the Approved Budget for the Contract to
avoid incurrence of unreasonable costs of contracts pursuant to
Annex “A”, Statement 3(f) of the Revised IRR of R.A. 9184.

9.6 We recommended that Management ensure that goods purchased be
satisfactorily delivered within the specified schedule in the contract or
PO to avoid liquidated damages that maybe imposed in accordance
with Annex D of RA No. 9184.

10.7 We recommended that management:
a. stop the practice of issuing gasoline as a donation or assistance.
This kind of transaction naturally belongs to and is inherent in
the CSWDO whose official functions cover the granting of
various types of assistance to needy constituents under any of its
planned programs for the year.
b. limit the issuance of gasoline to vehicles owned by the City in
accordance with the aforementioned regulation since as of this
reporting the city have motor vehicles sufficient to be used for
official business. Likewise, consider reimbursement of actual
transportation incurred in the performance of official duties for
official transactions using their private vehicles.

11.6 We recommended that the Local Chief Executive observe the provisions
of RA No. 7160 in giving authority to LGU officials to perform duties or
function in his behalf particularly on the approval of disbursement
vouchers.

12.11 We recommended that the LCE require the BAC to submit contracts
and supporting documents to the Auditor for review and evaluation
within five days after perfection thereof so that errors and omissions
discovered during the review shall be rectified immediately before the
actual completion/consummation of said transaction and to avoid: a)
repetition of errors for succeeding related transactions of the City, b)
administrative sanctions against erring personnel.

13.4. We recommended that management enforce prompt settlement of
suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 and
7.1.2 of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts
(RRSA).

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the imposition of
appropriate liquidated damages, forfeiture of performance bond, if
necessary, pursuant to the provisions of the Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised
IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure contractor’s faithful compliance of its
contractual obligations and to minimize/eliminate delay in the implementation
of projects.

The City Engineering Office assures the conduct of close monitoring of
project implementation to ensure their timely completion. Likewise, further
ensure that liquidated damages, if any, shall be properly imposed against

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that management:
a. effect payment of goods and services through checks payable to the
suppliers or contractor except on audit exceptional cases where needed
items are extremely necessary and urgent which cannot be conveniently
paid by check;
b. grant cash advance to the city cashier/disbursing officer for the payment of
prizes, honoraria and labor payroll;
c. conduct earlier and thorough planning of an activity particularly the
implementation of regular activities or occasions to allow enough time for
needed procurement and likewise, avoid granting cash advances for
purposes not among those indicated in COA Circular 97-002; and
d. demand collection of overdue cash advances particularly those
pertaining to prior years or request for write off if determined uncollectible.
observations.managementAction:
The City complied to the recommendation. Fully implemented actions were
undertaken for payments through checks and planning improvements. The
balance of cash advances amounting to P 118,834.00 pertaining to prior years
remained unsettled as of year end, and no request for write-off were
submitted to the audit team.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the city government, through its GAD Focal Point
System (GFPS), should consider the following to ensure compliance with the
Part 4. C of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No.
2013-01 dated July 18, 2013:
a. Prepare realistic budget allocation for Gender and Development (GAD)
programs, activities and projects that will allow minimal and acceptable cost
variance upon implementation; and
b. Conduct thorough monitoring on the implementation of Gender and
Development (GAD) programs, activities and projects to ensure their
realization at year-end.
observations.managementAction:
The City Government will comply with the recommendations and directed
the GAD Monitoring and Evaluation Team to conduct thorough monitoring
to ensure their timely implementation.

observations.recommendation:

authorized official in compliance with Section 8.2 of DBM NBC Circular No.
446 dated November 24, 1995 and return to the General Services Office
excess units issued for the cancellation of the corresponding AREs;
b. recall all motorcycles issued to officials and employees whose work
designation does not use and warrant any field work and usage in remote
areas;
c. provide a city’s motorpool to keep all other motor vehicles for common use
which will be made available upon request by other personnel on official
business; and
d. create a committee to craft internal policy guidelines to be approved for
adoption in the maintenance of motorpool and the use of motor vehicles
under the responsibility of a designated custodian.
observations.managementAction:
There are plans to recall excess motor vehicles and update AREs; plans for
a motor pool and policy crafting are not yet implemented.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City Treasury Office:
a. require from business establishments in the assessment of business taxes
the submission of a copy of their audited financial statements and/ or
Certified Income Tax Returns in the preceding year duly received by the
Bureau of Internal Revenue. Any discrepancy between the declared revenue
and the financial statements will be evaluated and assessed accordingly; and
b. require the BPLO and the Evaluator or the person In-charge in the
assessment of the tax to properly check/review the gross sales/receipts
declared by the taxpayer, if warranted and randomly examine the books of
accounts of businessmen in order to check the propriety of the declared gross
receipts which are the bases for tax assessment.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer explained that the submission of Financial Statements
(FS) are not yet available in January when business permits are being
issued; hence, the Executive Order of the former mayor to use FS of the next
preceding year as basis for tax assessment. However, the submission of the
next FS which is due on April 30 of the year was no longer required by the
Treasury Office, thus no more re-computation done to determine any
discrepancy between the declared revenue and the FS.

annual budget.

Despite best efforts made by the CSWM through their various programs and

non-utilization of treatment facility due to its on-going rehabilitation.

observations.recommendation:
1.10. We recommended that the City Accountant:
a. Prepare and maintain Property, Plant and Equipment (PPE) - Ledger Cards for all public infrastructures recognized in the books of accounts indicating cost of each component pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2015-008, and 2016-004 which shall be the basis in computing the related depreciation expenses; and
b. Ensure that derecognition of public infrastructure accounts are properly taken-up in the books of accounts prior to the recording of their replacement costs due to betterments or improvements, in accordance with PPSAS No. 17.

3.8. We recommended that management implement all programs, activities, and projects in accordance with its Approved Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan (LDRRMFIP) to enhance the city’s disaster preparedness and response capabilities in times of calamity.

4.8. We recommended that the Solid Waste Management Program Committee prepare policy/guidelines on the operation of the machineries acquired for adoption by the City Government to ensure their optimal utilization and proper disposition of finished products.

5.6. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure that all contracts are executed only at its effectivity date or upon receipt of the Notice to Proceed by the Contractor/Supplier pursuant to the Governing Principles on Government Procurement and Section 37.4 of the 2016 Revised Implementing Rules and Regulations (IRR) of Republic Act No. 9184.

6.7. We recommended that the Local Chief Executive ensure the imposition of appropriate liquidated damages, forfeiture of performance bond, if necessary, pursuant to the provisions of the Annex “E” of the 2016 Revised IRR of RA No. 9184, to ensure contractor’s faithful compliance of its contractual obligations and to minimize/eliminate delay in the implementation of projects.

7.8. We recommended that management avoid references to brand names and tailor-fitting of specifications and advise the BAC and Technical Working Group to revisit the Local Government Procurement Manual to comply with the provisions of the RA No. 9184 in the procurement of goods.
8.6. We recommended that management adhere strictly with Section 3.3 of the DILG Memorandum Circular No. 2014-155 dated December 14, 2014 in the procurement of motor vehicles.

9.19. We recommended that the BAC:
a. carefully examine and verify all the documents submitted and recompute, if possible, data provided by the bidders to determine whether they comply with and are responsive to all the requirements and conditions specified in the Bidding Documents; and
b. revisit the Local Government Procurement Manual to properly comply with the provisions of RA No. 9184.

10.14. We recommended that:
a. The City Inventory, Asset Management, Appraisal and Disposal Committee conduct thorough and proper appraisal in determining the just and reasonable price to be offered to owners of real property/ies to be acquired; and
b. The City Mayor draw and execute Contract of Sale on acquisition of real properties containing the obligations of both parties such as, but not limited to: terms of payment until title is finally transferred and payment of taxes and other fees, in order to protect the interest of the government.

11.4. We recommended that the Sangguniang Panglungsod and the City Mayor re-assess or evaluate their plans and decisions on the operations of the city government to ensure that government funds are utilized for programs, projects, and activities that are most beneficial to the constituents.

13.9. We recommended that the:
a. City Mayor liquidate cash advance for confidential and/or intelligence fund within 30 days after the end of the quarter and ensure that cash advances for the same purpose are used during the targeted period of program/project implementation;
b. City Accountant issue certification that previous cash advance for intelligence and/or confidential fund has been liquidated only upon receipt by the COA- Intelligence and Confidential Fund Audit Office of the duly transmitted liquidation reports; and
c. City Accountant ensure the timely submission of cash advance vouchers for Confidential Fund together with the complete/ pertinent supporting documents pursuant to Joint Circular No. 2015-01 of the COA, DBM, DILG, Governance Commission of the GOCC, and DND.

15.9. We recommended that the city government, through its GAD Focal Point System (GFPS), should consider the following to ensure compliance with the Part 4. C of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 dated July 18, 2013:
a. Prepare realistic budget allocation for Gender and Development (GAD) programs, activities and projects that will allow minimal and acceptable cost variance upon implementation; and
b. Conduct thorough monitoring on the implementation of Gender and Development (GAD) programs, activities and projects to ensure their realization at year-end.

16.9. We recommended that management establish guidelines or procedures as provided for in DBM-DSWD Joint Circular No. 1, Series of 2018 to clearly identify personnel assigned/duration of exposure to hazardous areas or exposed to occupational risk in order to support entitlement to hazard pay.

17.5. We recommended that:
a. The General Services Office prepare Inventory and Inspection Report for Unserviceable Properties (IIRUP) to be submitted to the audit team for evaluation;
b. The Appraisal and Disposal Committee conduct an appraisal on the unserviceable motor vehicles and facilitate the disposal in accordance with applicable rules and regulations; and
c. The Accounting Office draw the necessary journal entries to derecognize the unserviceable motor vehicles after the disposal.
19.4. We recommended and Management agreed to update ARE of all properties for easy monitoring and reconciliation.

20.5. We recommended and management agreed to enforce prompt settlement of suspensions and disallowances in accordance with Section 7.1.1 and 7.1.2 of the 2009 Rules and Regulations on Settlement of Accounts (RRSA).

Management update the City‟s
Revenue Ordinance to be able to
expand its coverage, adjust and update
rates and simplify provisions suited to
the present economic condition in the
locality.
observations.managementAction:
The inability of the legislative
body to immediately act on the
draft Revenue Code upon receipt
in CY 2018 had left the same
unacted as of CY 2019.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the City
Treasury Office:
a. require from business establishments in
the assessment of business taxes the
submission of a copy of their audited
financial statements and/ or Certified
Income Tax Returns in the preceding
year duly received by the Bureau of
Internal Revenue. Any discrepancy
between the declared revenue and the
financial statements will be evaluated
and assessed accordingly; and
b. require the BPLO and the Evaluator or
the person In-charge in the assessment
of the tax to properly check/review the
gross sales/receipts declared by the
taxpayer, if warranted and randomly
examine the books of accounts of
businessmen in order to check the
propriety of the declared of gross
receipts which are the bases for tax
assessment.
observations.managementAction:
The City Treasurer explained that
Financial Statements (FS) are not yet
available in January when business
permits are being issued, hence the
Executive Order of the former mayor
to use FS of the next preceding year
as basis for tax assessment. However,
the submission of the next FS which
is due on April 30 of the year was no
longer required by the Treasury
Office, thus no more re-computation
done to determine any discrepancy
between the declared revenue and the
FS.

Random assessment and review had
been conducted by the BPLO in CY
2019 which had partly attributed to
23.96% increase of collections for CY
2019.

observations.recommendation:

council monitor all departments‟
submission of GAD Accomplishment
Reports (AR) with the corresponding
actual costs for particular programs/
projects to enable us to promptly
evaluate the effectiveness of the
programs and the efficiency in the
utilization of government funds
allotted for the purpose.

for CY 2019 were submitted to the

website.

₱69,170.00 or 8.06% of the total
disallowances was settled during the
year.

Reiterated in Observation No. 20
Part II of this report.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management prescribe a policy on the use and maintenance of all government service vehicles of the city.  The policy should be in compliance with COA Circular No. 77-61, dated September 26, 1977 and may include the following:

- provision on pool system to be implemented by the General Services Office;

- the process of request for use and the approving authority; and

  -the charging of gasoline and other related expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management did not issue more than one vehicle to some officers of the City, except those in-charged with the City ENRO, City Motorpool and City DRRM office.
observations.reason: Management may not have issued more than one vehicle to some officers of the City, however, no policy was prescribed on the use and maintenance of all government service vehicles of the city. In the exit conference, Management committed to draft policy on the use of the vehicles of the City.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the City Accountant record the necessary adjustment in the books of the LGU to reflect the correct balances of the affected accounts.

We have recommended  that the Local Chief Executive:  (a) create an Inventory Committee to conduct  physical count of the local roads network; and (b) require  all offices concerned to complete the description and cost segregation of road projects  as basis in recording their cost or valuation in the books of the city.

We have recommended that Management:
prioritize and fast track the distribution of the remaining relief goods especially those food items soon to expire to prevent further wastage of the goods; and
arrange stock piles in an orderly manner, segregating perishable goods, medicines and medical supplies from the LDRRM equipment and other dry goods for distribution, maintain bin cards and keeptrack of expiry dates of the goods.

We have recommended that the CSWD Officer, the General Services Officer and the City Accountant comply with COA Circular No. 2014-002 dated April 15, 2014 in reporting of receipts, utilization, distribution and status of relief goods.

We have recommended that:

Management issue demand letters to borrowers in default for the immediate payment of their loans;

the concerned department or the project implementers review the loan agreements and re-validate the existence of the loan borrowers and loan amounts; and

Management re-assess their collection policies to include sanctions and remedies to be imposed to borrowers with past due loans.

We have recommended that Management update the City’s Revenue Ordinance to be able to expand its coverage, adjust and update rates and simplify provisions suited to the present economic condition in the locality.

We have recommended that the City Treasury Office:
require from business establishments in the assessment of business taxes  the submission of a copy of their audited financial statements and/ or Certified Income Tax Returns in the preceding year duly received by the Bureau of Internal Revenue. Any discrepancy between the declared revenue and the financial statements will be evaluated and assessed accordingly; and
require the BPLO and the Evaluator or the person In-charge in the assessment of the tax to properly check/review the gross sales/receipts declared by the taxpayer, if warranted and randomly examine the books of accounts of businessmen in order to check the propriety of the declared of gross receipts which are the bases for tax assessment.

We have recommended that the GFPS council monitor all departments’ submission of GAD Accomplishment Reports (AR) with the corresponding actual costs for particular programs/projects to enable us to promptly evaluate the effectiveness of the programs and the efficiency in the utilization of government funds allotted for the purpose.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that LGU officials and employees be required to refund the total amount of ₱3,755,500.00 representing overpayment of their PEIs.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the City Government prepare and approve a policy guideline on the granting of donation\s to individuals, barangays, and associations, and attached national government agencies to include its documentary requirements, among others. 

Further, we have recommended that Management:

Create a committee to evaluate requests for financial assistance charged to donations to ensure that such are valid and proper;

Require the recipient barangays and attached national government agencies of donated equipment to show proof that said equipment are duly accounted for in their books; and

See to it that the grant of the financial assistance to LGUs/POs/NGAs are supported by a Memorandum of Agreement (MOA) and accounted first as a receivable (Due from LGUs/POs/NGAs) which shall be subject to liquidation to ensure that these are used for purposes for which these were granted.

Moreover, we have recommended that requests for financial assistance for the celebration of fiesta, for purchase of uniforms in joining sports fests and the like addressed to the elective officials be treated as personal solicitations to be funded out of their representation allowance.
observations.managementAction: No Management action was undertaken to comply with the recommendation

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management undertake efficient and aggressive tax collection efforts and develop strategies to improve tax collection efficiency. Likewise, management should seek the assistance of the barangay officials in the dissemination and distribution of notices or orders of [payment of delinquent taxes to taxpayers within their territorial jurisdiction to boost collections. Avail of the remedies available under Section 256 of R.A. No. 7160, if necessary.
observations.managementAction: Management cannot impose additional tax collection remedies for the collection of RPT until such time that the City Council approves the Revenue Code of the City.
observations.reason: The increasing trend in RPT and SEF Receivables implies that the city had not made any action to improve its collection efficiency.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that management assess its existing organizational structure so that ideal number of plantilla personnel may be established, thereby, avoiding the hiring of excessive casuals and job order of employees. Ensure that the hiring of casuals and job orders should only be resorted based on necessity in order to avoid waste of much needed funds for more priority programs of the City government.
observations.managementAction: Management commented that the hiring of job orders is  necessary .
observations.reason: The 634 Job Orders still composes the largest chunk of the existing manpower  of the city  which is 167%  of the 379 approved regular plantilla.

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the City Accountant maintain the required special accounts – Public Market, Slaughterhouse and Cemetery in the General Fund as prescribed under Section 105 of the New Government Accounting System (NGAS) manual for LGU Volume 1.
observations.managementAction: These operations are heavily subsidized by the General Fund thus, it cannot be classified as a Local Economic Enterprise
observations.reason: The recommendation is not long doable. Income generated by the Public Market and Slaughterhouse are minimal and not sufficient to fund its own operations.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that management submit immediately the copies of contracts and their supporting documents to the auditor for review and custody. Henceforth submit the required documents within five (5) working days after perfection thereof in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-001.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant take up in the books of accounts the agricultural products with estimated cost of ₱527,700.00 in accordance with the Revised Chart of Accounts for Local Government Units prescribed under COA Circular No. 2005-009 and to adjust the balance of the Agricultural and Forestry Equipment by ₱2,200,000.00.

We recommended that management procure their requirements for common use supplies from the PS-DBM for the best interest of the government and to resort to alternative modes of procurement in cases where the said items are not available at the PS-DBM.

We recommended that management observe the prohibition on the display of picture, motto and other representation on signboards and other publicity materials relating to any PPA associated with the top leadership.

We recommended that an official receipt be issued for every collection derived from the operations of the tractors and that all collections therefrom be deposited promptly and intact. Further, all expenditures for the maintenance and operations of the tractors should pass through the regular process where disbursement vouchers are prepared with complete supporting documents.

We further recommended that an initial inventory of animals and other agricultural products at the Balong-Agro Center be conducted and that a report thereon be prepared for proper recording and accounting. Management shall thereafter prepare all other required reports such as the reports on sales, disposal, live births and death.
We recommended that the special accounts be maintained in the General Fund in accordance with the requirements provided for under the New Government Accounting System for Local Government Units.
We recommended that management adhere to the provisions of COA Circular No. 2007-001 and Section 36 of RA 7160 on the release, utilization, reporting and the accounting of funds granted to NGOs/POs.

We recommended that the utilization of LDRRMF be limited to disaster risk management activities relative to prevention and mitigation, preparedness, response, reconstruction and rehabilitation, as well as procurement of basic rescue and response equipment, in accordance with existing laws and regulations.

We recommended that the LDRRMO include in the City’s LDRRMFIP the list of projects and activities, in a separate caption, to be charged to the unexpended balance of the fund in previous years and for the Municipal Accountant to prepare the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF on a monthly basis in compliance with regulations.
We recommended that management create a GAD M&E Team who shall evaluate the outcomes of the LGU GAD policies, programs and projects pursuant to Item 5.1 of the PCW-DILG-DBM-NEDA JMC No. 2013-01.

We recommended that management exhaust all available remedies provided by law and regulations in enforcing settlement of the audit disallowances in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006 dated September 15, 2009.

We recommended that management include honoraria received by employees as part of the employees’ gross compensation income, withhold the correct amount of tax and include these data in the Annual Information Return of Income Taxes Withheld on Compensation and Final Withholding Taxes.

We recommended that:

the City Accounting Office submit schedule to support the account Due to GSIS amounting to P3,157,346.25 as of December 31, 2016; and

the City promptly remit all GSIS employees’ and government contributions including loan amortizations withheld pursuant to RA No. 8291, otherwise known as the “Government Service Insurance System Act of 1997”.

We recommended that the agency conduct an annual inventory of its Property, Plant and Equipment and submit the required report to the auditor not later than January 31 of each year. Likewise, PPE Ledger Cards should be maintained for an effective handling/control of PPE account balances reflected in the financial statements.
observations.managementAction: Physical Inventory Taking was conducted by the personnel of the City GSO and report was duly submitted to the audit team for evaluation.
observations.reason: Physical inventory was conducted by the City GSO. However, the result of the Inventory taking cannot be reconciled with the booked Property, Plant and Equipment due to the absence of Property Ledger Cards to be maintained by the Accounting Department.

observations.recommendation:
We recommended that Management:

Require the accountable officers to immediately liquidate their cash advances, otherwise, appropriate charges should be filed against them for malversation of public funds and failure to render accounts; and

Strictly comply with the rules and regulations in the grant, utilization and liquidation of cash advances as provided for under COA Circular No. 97-002 dated February 10, 1997.
observations.managementAction: Settlement through salary deduction was made for cash advances aged two years and above.
observations.reason: As of December 31, 2016, ₱1,405,044.56 or 47% of the total outstanding cash advances pertain to previous years’ issuances.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that Management ensure the proper designation and bonding of additional collecting officers pursuant to Section 101 (2) of P.D. 1445 and Section 305 (f) of R.A. 7160. Otherwise all personnel acting as collectors without duly approved bond should be ordered immediately stop from acting as such and all accountable forms and collections in their custody be remitted to the Liquidating Officer
observations.managementAction: Additional collectors were already duly designated and bonded.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that the City ensure that at least 5 percent of its total appropriation is set aside for the implementation of GAD related projects and activities to address the various gender issues in the City as required in Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 of the PCW-DILG-DBM and NEDA. Likewise, an overall GAD Plan Budget should be prepared to support the allocation of the required amount of GAD budget for the year. 

We have likewise recommended that the LGU facilitate the creation of the LGU GAD Focal Point System who shall be tasked to prepare and submit the required annual GAD Plan and Budget of the City.
observations.managementAction:
Management identified programs, projects and activities related to GAD in its 2016 Annual Budget

An unnumbered Executive Order dated June 17, 2015 creating the GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) was issued.
observations.reason: Management identified programs, projects and activities related to GAD in its 2016 Annual Budget

observations.recommendation: We have recommended that the management strictly comply with the provisions of COA Circular No. 2009-01 dated February 12, 2009, particularly on the furnishing of the copies of perfected contracts and purchase orders within five (5) working days upon perfection/execution thereof to the office of the auditor. Otherwise, the penalty prescribed by the law under the Sec. 4.1 of the Circular will be enforced.

observations.recommendation:
We have recommended that management prescribe a policy on the use and maintenance of all government service vehicles of the City. The policy should be in compliance with COA Circular No.77-61, dated September 26, 1977 and may include the following:

Provision on pool system to be implemented by the General services Office;
The process of request for use and approving authority; and
The charging of gasoline and other related expenses.
observations.managementAction: Management issued other vehicles to other personnel of the LGU
observations.reason: City Officials were limited to one motor vehicle each, however, there was no policy formulated on the use and maintenance thereof.
